title: iris graminea subsp. sylvatica
intro: Iris graminea subsp. sylvatica is a sub-species of the wild iris, a flowering plant that belongs to the iris family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and antiseptic properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Iris graminea subsp. sylvatica include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs and colds, and even help to lower blood pressure. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
